title: Logan Dillon
content:
People.ContactEmail ljedillon@mccarthy.ca
Logan recently completed his JD at Queen’s University Faculty of Law. While at Queen’s, Logan was a student caseworker at the Queen’s Elder Law Clinic providing pro bono legal services and acted as the Vice President of Finance for the Queen’s Indigenous Law Students’ Alliance. Logan worked as a research assistant in his final year, conducting research to help establish an Indigenous legal services project in the Kingston community.
In the course of his JD at Queen’s Law, Logan received the Queen’s Law Course Prize in Property Law, as well as a Course Prize in Trial Advocacy awarded for Best Team.
Logan completed an International Business Law Certificate at Bader College in East Sussex, England. While completing this certificate, Logan participated in a small-scale International Trade Law MOOT in Geneva, Switzerland, as well as chaired for a trade-negotiation simulation.
Before starting law school, Logan graduated with Distinction from Carleton University having completed his Bachelor of Arts Honours in Law with a Concentration in Business Law. During this time, Logan worked for the Government of Canada at the Department of National Defence as a student. While studying at Carleton, he was an active member of the Carleton Model United Nations team.
Outside of Law, Logan maintains an active lifestyle through bird watching, and enjoys the game of chess both as a student and a player.
